title: Thiollierea neriifolia
intro: Thiollierea neriifolia, also known as the " Nerium-leaved Thiollierea", is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Thiollierea neriifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Thiollierea neriifolia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in capsule or extract form for modern herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Thiollierea neriifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Thiollierea, which is a group of plants endemic to New Caledonia. The species is further classified as a member of the order Gentianales. Within the family Rubiaceae, Thiollierea neriifolia is part of the subfamily Rubioideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
